title: Frog Position After T Seconds
hints: Model the frog's movement as a random walk on the tree. Since it never revisits nodes, the path is unique once the sequence of neighbors is chosen.
Use dynamic programming where the state is the current vertex and the time elapsed. Transition probabilities depend on the number of unvisited neighbors at each step.
If the frog reaches a leaf before time t, it stays there. Thus, the probability accumulates at leaves and persists until time t.
